title: Deployments, ReplicaSets, Rollouts, Strategies, Conditions, and Recovery · Platform Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan for deployments, replicasets, rollouts, strategies, conditions, and recovery. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of observable Deployment rollout and recovery plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Deployment template hash, ReplicaSet ownership, desired replicas, Pod replacement, adoption boundaries, and observed status.
Calculate RollingUpdate maxSurge, maxUnavailable, total capacity, readiness, termination, and rollout duration under a changed replica count.
Bind Pod template changes to immutable image digests, configuration identity, pull policy, provenance, and selected platform evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Deployment rolls out image tag latest, stalls at 3 unavailable replicas, exceeds progress deadline, and has a bad ConfigMap change outside revision history. Bind immutable identity, strategy, conditions, pause, rollback, and verification. Competency probe: Trace Deployment template hash, ReplicaSet ownership, desired replicas, Pod replacement, adoption boundaries, and observed status.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace deployment template hash, replicaset ownership, desired replicas, pod replacement, adoption boundaries, and observed status. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership, keep K8s Deployment Replicaset Ownership connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Rollback Causal Recovery
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-rollback-causal-recovery-1frxrc". Tailor its deployment.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Rollback Causal Recovery.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Rollback Causal Recovery into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Rollback Causal Recovery, keep K8s Rollback Causal Recovery connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-rollback-causal-recovery-1frxrc
  - competency: k8s-rollback-causal-recovery
    artifact: deployment.yaml
    decision: rollback causal recovery
    environment: single-node minikube profile with a constrained Docker driver
    fault-injection: rotate a projected token while the API dependency slows
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: 1frxrc
    change: |
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      replicas: 4
      strategy: {maxSurge: 1, maxUnavailable: 0}
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
    verify: kubectl rollout status deployment/status-api && kubectl get rs,pods -l app=status-api -o json
    failure: Reject mutable release identity, readiness-free rollout success, capacity arithmetic errors, or rollback presented as external-state recovery.
    repair: Bind image and config identity, calculate surge and unavailable capacity, inspect conditions and ReplicaSets, and coordinate roll forward or rollback.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Rollback Causal Recovery

Write the workshop evidence note for K8s Rollback Causal Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop Deployment rolls out image tag latest, stalls at 2 unavailable replicas, exceeds progress deadline, and has a bad ConfigMap change outside revision history. steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Rollback Causal Recovery, keep K8s Rollback Causal Recovery connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose roll forward or rollback from causal evidence while coordinating schemas, config, traffic, durable state, compatibility, and post-recovery verification.
